string.text: Skip the courtship ladder — marry an eligible couple immediately.
Off (default): convene the council yourself via the Master Herald. On: your Heralds also send a brief periodic digest (warnings + a pointer to the full report) on the interval below.
Skip Exchequer income mutation when Banner Kings is loaded. Default on.
Disable Exchequer income bonus regardless of detection. Overrides Conservative.
Increases all office bonuses by 50%. Exclusive with the other Bonus Strength rows.
Halves all office bonuses. Exclusive with the other Bonus Strength rows.
Extra companion slots at clan tier 0. Default 4 (the four Herald offices). No artificial ceiling.
Additional companion slots per clan tier (added to the base bonus).
Apply safety caps on overlapping mods (Banner Kings, Improved Garrisons) even when not detected. Off = Automatic detection.
Success chance when a married couple tries for an heir.
How often the automatic council digest convenes when enabled above. Default 21 days (about a season).

Default off. Off (Force): HH delivers the Exchequer income bonus through a clan-finance override that wraps and preserves any other finance mod and adds the bonus on top, identically on PC and Steam Deck. On (Defer): if another mod overrides the clan finance model, HH yields to it and the Exchequer income bonus does not apply. Takes effect on the next campaign load.
Defer Exchequer income to another finance mod
Daily-tick re-validate Clan Home ownership. Catches DF-brokered settlement transfers.

Off (default): big-deal actions (intrigue, herald appointments, changing the clan seat) can only be done remotely once your clan reaches Tier 5 — at home they're always available. On: remote access to those needs no tier (use the herald from anywhere).
Off by default. Welcome a household member (consort) from a tavern; gift/romance/children; part with them at any time. Clan role-play; never gated behind any supporter feature.
Master switch for romance, marriage, mentoring, heirs, and household members. Off = vanilla family behaviour.
Recruit local commoners into service (Muster) and turn captured brigands to your colours via the Ransom Broker (Watch). Routes through the offices; recruits are ordinary clan companions.
Disabling hides intrigue + military Master menu actions. Administrative + status reads remain.
On first load after upgrade, show a one-time dialog explaining what migrated. Future versions only.
Master kill-switch. Disabling stops all bonuses and warnings; existing roster + Clan Home state are preserved.
Ask a married couple to try for an heir (a chance to fail) and send a spouse a gift for relation.
Free a captive enemy lord held in your party and bring them into your clan as a full lord. Routed through the Watch.
Induct a same-culture rebel lord who lost their seat AND is still leading a party (party-less landless rebels are removed by the game before you could reach them). Routed through the Muster.
Court and marry eligible adults through the Master Herald; assign a mentor for a daily skill-XP exchange.
Veterans that accumulate enough battle merit can be ennobled into clan companions via the Master Herald (Household menu).
Skip Muster/Arms garrison mutations when Improved Garrisons is loaded. Default on.
Disable Muster + Arms garrison effects entirely. Party effects remain.

Add extra recruitable-companion slots to your clan (a flat bonus on top of the vanilla tier limit), and free the slot of each companion you station as a herald so they do not count against your recruit limit. If TrueLimits is installed and Limits mode is Auto, this steps aside entirely.
Add to the number of parties your clan can field (companion-led parties on the map).
Add to your MAIN party's troop cap. Player-only by design — AI/lord parties keep vanilla sizes for game balance.
Add to your MAIN party's prisoner cap. Player-only by design.

Off: never change any limit. On: apply the limits below. With 'Force over other limit mods' OFF this is Auto mode — we defer to a detected limit mod (e.g. TrueLimits) and only apply when none is present.
Off (default) = Auto: defer to a detected limit mod (TrueLimits) and apply ours only when none is present. On = Forced: always apply ours, STACKING on top of any other limit mod.
Daily cap on relation gained from spouse/consort gifts.
Per-pair daily skill-XP granted to the mentee (in the mentor's strongest skill). 0 disables the mentor tick.
Fraction of the mentee's grant the mentor also gains (also capped). 0 = one-way teaching.
Extra clan parties at tier 0. Multiple companion-led parties for the power-fantasy clan.
Additional clan parties per clan tier.
Extra main-party troop slots at clan tier 0. For a ~100-companion army set this high AND raise the companion limit.
Additional main-party troop slots per clan tier.
Extra main-party prisoner slots at clan tier 0.
Additional main-party prisoner slots per clan tier.
Gold to induct a captive lord. An active Watch Herald cuts it by a quarter.
Influence to induct a captive lord. An active Watch Herald cuts it by a quarter.
Kills a troop TYPE must accumulate in your party before one can be ennobled.
When on, higher-tier troops need proportionally more merit to promote (balances their greater starting power).
How much of the troop's skill values the new companion starts with. 100 = full veteran power.
Coin to convert each captured brigand into a companion. An active Watch Herald cuts the price by a quarter.
Maximum brigands you can convert in a single visit to the broker.
How much of the brigand's skills the convert keeps. They are fighters, so they keep more (60 by default).
Also offer landless minor-faction / mercenary nobles (not just rebels). Off by default.
Influence to induct a rebel/landless lord. An active Muster Herald cuts it by a quarter.
Only offer lords of your clan's culture. Off = any culture.
Show Recover Missing Heralds menu option
Wipes herald state in a save-safe way so the mod can be uninstalled. The Hero references go back to normal availability; nothing is killed or removed.
Coin to take a local commoner into service as a companion. An active Muster Herald cuts the price by a quarter.
How much of the local troop's skills the commoner starts with. Low-barrier = weak (25 by default).
How many days of ACTIVE service (leading a party, governing, running a caravan) one skill point of growth takes.
Active service: days per skill point
Caravan Master cap as a percentage of your effective companion limit (minimum 1 while enabled).
Castellan cap as a percentage of your effective companion limit (minimum 1 while enabled).
Commission growth stops at this skill level. Manual training past it is unaffected.
Flat boost granted to each of the track's skills at appointment. Reclaimed when the commission is revoked.
A holder not actively doing the job still grows, but this many times slower.
Marshal cap as a percentage of your effective companion limit (minimum 1 while enabled).

Commission companions as Marshal, Castellan, or Caravan Master: a flat skill package at appointment, growth while they serve, and a title. Revoking reclaims the package but keeps everything earned in service.

Ennoble veterans and grant/revoke commissions from any town or castle you visit, without a Master Herald. Its main effect is unlocking these before you own a fief; it also stays as portable field access afterward. Off: these route only through the seated Master Herald (land-first).
Lowest clan tier at which the field menu appears. 0 = from campaign start.
Field clan building: minimum clan tier
Skill levels each picked skill gains in round 1. Later rounds give less (round 2 is half, round 3 a third), like Distinguished Service. Excess-kill points add on top, capped at +20.
Extra build budget for each kill the type earned beyond the threshold - reward the truly distinguished.
Skill build: points per excess kill
How many veterans you can ennoble in one review. Over-killing a troop type banks several elevations, so you can promote more than one of the same type at once (like Distinguished Service).
How many skills you pick each round of the skill build.
When on, you hand-pick skills to boost when ennobling a veteran (a Distinguished Service style skill build), on top of the inherited skills. Off: the companion just keeps its inherited troop skills.
How many rounds of picking skills you get per ennobled veteran.
Auto-pick the skill build instead of prompting - useful when promoting several at once.
